full_text:
HRD consensus project: PARPi cohort results Project meeting 21.5.2025 Matilda Salko

109 patients in total
All PARPi treated

Clinical characteristics of the PARPi subcohort

PARPi cohort: clinical details

Baseline characteristics of the retrospective PARPi cohort | n=109
Median age at diagnosis | 63 (range 40-85)
Stage | 
I | 3 (2.8%)
II | 2 (1.8%)
III | 71 (65.1%)
IV | 32 (29.4%)
unknown | 1 (0.9%)
Histology | 
HGSC | 104 (95%)
HGSC & ca endometrioides endometrii | 1 (0.9%)
LGSC | 1 (0.9%)
ca endometrioides ovarii G3 | 2 (1.8%)
ca seromucinosum | 1 (0.9%)

8.3% (n=9) discontinued the treatment due to adverse effects
1.8% (n=2) were diagnosed with MDS after PARPi treatment
1 patient was also diagnosed with acute leukemia after PARPi

Median PARPi treatment duration
Whole cohort 9.17 months

BRCAwt patients (n=64) 5.31 months
BRCAmut patients (n=45) 24.0 months
PARPi duration BRCAwt vs. BRCAmut p<0.0001 (Mann-Whitney U test)

PARPi cohort: clinical details

PARPi treatment

Median PFS after PARPi treatment was initiated
Whole cohort 10.6 months

BRCAwt patients (n=64) 5.82 months
BRCAmut patients (n=45) 34.1 months

PARPi cohort: survival

Survival

Median OS
Whole cohort 65.0 months

BRCAwt patients (n=64) 63.8 months
BRCAmut patients (n=45) 72.6 months

94 samples from 93 patients were sent for SNParray in Tartu
All samples had sufficient amount of DNA for SNParray (>30 ng/ul)
Tumor purity
Tumor purity of <30%, n=8
Unknown tumor purity, n=4
Samples were collected between 2011-2021

PARPi cohort: samples

SNParray samples

Tumor purity did not explain failures

…and neither did the sample age

ASCAT failed to call results in 35% of the samples (n=33)
Why?

Sample DNA quality?

PARPi cohort: samples

Of the 61 samples that we got a result from
3.3% (n=2) returned 0s for all 3 parameters, making ovaHRDscar 0
Tumor purity 10% and 35%, respectively
29.5% (n=18) returned 0s for either LOH, LST or TAI

Similar to ASCAT failures, tumor purity did not explain 0-values

0s

QCs: MAPD

In general, this sample set had poorer MAPD values compared to previously acquired FFPE samples
Sample tumor purity and DNA concentration were not generally worse compared to previous sample sets
DNA quality?

PARPi cohort: samples

QCs: CallRate

37.2% of samples meet the CallRate criteria used for FF samples (CallRate> 0.8)
Generally better results than for MAPD

We have focused on MAPD for FFPE samples and have not (yet) explored CallRate thresholds

PARPi cohort: samples

We have previously observed that LOH is always the first parameter to become 0
In this sample set however, LST or TAI could be 0 even when LOH > 0

PARPi cohort: samples

I will further explore this next, and calculate the absolute nLOH values

PARPi cohort: clinical data

Applying the HRD results from Tartu to the cohort

Median PARPi treatment duration
HRD patients (n=16) 22.9 months
HRP patients (n=51) 6.67 months
PARPi duration HRP vs. HRD p=0.08 (Mann-Whitney U test)

PARPi cohort: survival

Applying the HRD results from Tartu to the cohort

Median PFS after PARPi
HRD patients (n=16) 26.4 months
HRP patients (n=51) 7.53 months
PFS HRP vs. HRD p=0.01 (Mann-Whitney U test)

Median OS
HRD patients (n=16) 60.5 months
HRP patients (n=51) 65.0 months
OS HRP vs. HRD p=0.48 (Mann-Whitney U test)

Questions to address and next steps
